summary:
The case reflects patients' resistance due to misperceptions about risk or benefit, aligning closely with the Health Belief Model’s focus on perceived susceptibility, severity, benefits, barriers, and self-efficacy. This makes the HBM the most suitable theoretical foundation among available models for explaining non-compliance in this context.

We are asked only to summarize the **agent's reasoning**, not rephrase the entire content into two sentences as if writing an original summary.

Final Summary:  
TheHealth Belief Model explains patient reluctance through perceptions of susceptibility, severity, benefits, barriers, and self-efficacy—key factors present when individuals ignore screening advice despite education and reminders. Thus, it is the best fit because these psychological constructs directly address why behavior change fails despite interventions.
